title: ［突發］入境處拒批英藉國際學校老師visa 10日內離港

replies.msg:
https://www.facebook.com/223783954322429/posts/4124987234202062/?d=n

繼續加速
【#Patreon突發時空🇭🇰🇬🇧】友人傳來以下資訊：有國際學校的英籍英文教師被通知要10日內離開香港，因為工作簽證被政府拒絕續約。而政府的原因，是指香港失業率很高，所以不能再讓外籍人士爭奪本地工作機會，加上外籍英文老師並非必需品。據說這並非個別例子，不少國際學校、和要續約的英籍教師，都面對類似情況，這在舊香港從未有過......
非必需，英文非必需，跟隨祖國步伐
國際學校好快同普通學校無分別
老師：原本我plan 一年內走人，依家加速到10日
又唔係佢嘅錢
愛國者以不懂英文為榮。
普通話老師就一大批一大批
加速啲外資走，仲邊有鬼佬敢帶家人嚟香港打工

香港失業率很高，所以不能再讓外籍人士爭奪本地工作機會

但又容許支那人嚟搶飯碗

有fd send嚟

Dear parents,

We are very sorry that the visa of Ms Maddie was rejected by Immigration last Monday and Immigration requires her to leave HK in 10 days.
Throughout the years, we have always succeeded in getting the visa of every single teacher. However, out of a huge shock, Ms Maddie’s visa extension was rejected with the reason that the unemployment rate in Hong Kong is historically high and a NET is not a must to teach English classes.

I would like to call for your understanding that we have never experienced such before. We also heard that many international schools in Hong Kong encountered similar problems lately, especially on the visa of UK teachers.

We have been trying our best to extend Ms Maddie’s period of stay in Hong Kong so as to facilitate better with the teacher transition in Steps. However, the Immigration Department still does not allow Ms Maddie to stay in HK to wait for the visa appeal results

quote.msg:
有fd send嚟

Dear parents,

We are very sorry that the visa of Ms Maddie was rejected by Immigration last Monday and Immigration requires her to leave HK in 10 days.
Throughout the years, we have always succeeded in getting the visa of every single teacher. However, out of a huge shock, Ms Maddie’s visa extension was rejected with the reason that the unemployment rate in Hong Kong is historically high and a NET is not a must to teach English classes.

I would like to call for your understanding that we have never experienced such before. We also heard that many international schools in Hong Kong encountered similar problems lately, especially on the visa of UK teachers.

We have been trying our best to extend Ms Maddie’s period of stay in Hong Kong so as to facilitate better with the teacher transition in Steps. However, the Immigration Department still does not allow Ms Maddie to stay in HK to wait for the visa appeal results

Dear parents,

We are very sorry that the visa of Ms Maddie was rejected by Immigration last Monday and Immigration requires her to leave HK in 10 days.
Throughout the years, we have always succeeded in getting the visa of every single teacher. However, out of a huge shock, Ms Maddie’s visa extension was rejected with the reason that the unemployment rate in Hong Kong is historically high and a NET is not a must to teach English classes.

I would like to call for your understanding that we have never experienced such before. We also heard that many international schools in Hong Kong encountered similar problems lately, especially on the visa of UK teachers.

We have been trying our best to extend Ms Maddie’s period of stay in Hong Kong so as to facilitate better with the teacher transition in Steps. However, the Immigration Department still does not allow Ms Maddie to stay in HK to wait for the visa appeal results

Dear parents,

We are very sorry that the visa of Ms Maddie was rejected by Immigration last Monday and Immigration requires her to leave HK in 10 days.
Throughout the years, we have always succeeded in getting the visa of every single teacher. However, out of a huge shock, Ms Maddie’s visa extension was rejected with the reason that the unemployment rate in Hong Kong is historically high and a NET is not a must to teach English classes.

I would like to call for your understanding that we have never experienced such before. We also heard that many international schools in Hong Kong encountered similar problems lately, especially on the visa of UK teachers.

We have been trying our best to extend Ms Maddie’s period of stay in Hong Kong so as to facilitate better with the teacher transition in Steps. However, the Immigration Department still does not allow Ms Maddie to stay in HK to wait for the visa appeal results

We also heard that many international schools in Hong Kong encountered similar problems lately, especially on the visa of UK teachers.

Dear parents,

We are very sorry that the visa of Ms Maddie was rejected by Immigration last Monday and Immigration requires her to leave HK in 10 days.

Throughout the years, we have always succeeded in getting the visa of every single teacher. However, out of a huge shock, Ms Maddie’s visa extension was rejected with the reason that the unemployment rate in Hong Kong is historically high and a NET is not a must to teach English classes.

I would like to call for your understanding that we have never experienced such before. We also heard that many international schools in Hong Kong encountered similar problems lately, especially on the visa of UK teachers.

We have been trying our best to extend Ms Maddie’s period of stay in Hong Kong so as to facilitate better with the teacher transition in Steps. However, the Immigration Department still does not allow Ms Maddie to stay in HK to wait for the visa appeal results.
